title: Epigastric Burning - Gastritis
chief_complaint: Epigastric burning pain for 1 month, worse with coffee and spicy foods, partially relieved by antacids. Occasional nausea. High stress job.

physical_exam.abdomen: Mild epigastric tenderness, no rebound, no guarding, bowel sounds normal

hidden_data.endoscopy: Erythematous gastric mucosa, no ulcer, no malignancy, biopsy shows chronic active gastritis

key_learning_points: H. pylori = most common cause of chronic gastritis

patient_responses.pain: It burns in my stomach, especially after coffee
patient_responses.stress: I work long hours and eat irregularly
patient_responses.antacids: Tums help a little but not completely

feedback.incorrect_message: This is H. pylori gastritis. The positive stool antigen and biopsy confirm it.

endoscopy.comment: Confirms gastritis and rules out ulcer/cancer.

feedback.attending_note: H. pylori gastritis: Triple therapy (PPI BID + Amoxicillin 1g BID + Clarithromycin 500mg BID) for 14 days. Confirm eradication with stool antigen 4 weeks after treatment. Counsel about triggers (coffee, spicy foods, stress).
